fr:mainmatter.fr:p: The type of isomorphisms onto C is inhabited, considering the identity isomorphism 1_{C}\colon C\cong C. Therefore, it suffices to show that the type of isomorphisms onto C depicted below is a proposition:
We unfold the definitions of a precategories and their isomorphisms, dropping propositional axioms:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {aligned} D_0&: \mathsf {Type}\\ D_1&:D_0\to D_0\to \mathsf {Set}\\ F_0&: D_0\to C_0\\ F_1&: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} D_1xy\to C \mathopen {}\left \lparen F_0x,F_0y\right \rparen \mathclose {} \\ h_1 &: \mathsf {isBiinvertible}\,F_0\\ h_2 &: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathsf {isPropMap} \mathopen {}\left \lparen F_1xy\right \rparen \mathclose {} \\ h_3 &: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathsf {isSurjection} \mathopen {}\left \lparen F_1xy\right \rparen \mathclose {} \end {aligned}
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: A function that is both propositional and a surjection, i.e. a bijection, is an equivalence by . We can therefore repackage our telescope equivalently:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {aligned} D_0&: \mathsf {Type}\\ D_1&:D_0\to D_0\to \mathsf {Set}\\ F_0&: D_0\cong C_0\\ F_1&: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} D_1xy\cong C \mathopen {}\left \lparen F_0x,F_0y\right \rparen \mathclose {} \end {aligned}

\begin {aligned} D_1&: \mathopen {}\left \lvert C\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathopen {}\left \lvert C\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathsf {Set}\\ F_1&: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in C\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in C\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in C\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in C\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} D_1xy\cong C \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\right \rparen \mathclose {} \end {aligned}
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Next we rewrite using the univalence principles for functions and families of types (, ):
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {aligned} D_1&: \mathopen {}\left \lvert C\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathopen {}\left \lvert C\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathsf {Set}\\ F_1&: D_1 = \lambda {x,y}\mathpunct {.}{C \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\right \rparen \mathclose {} } \end {aligned}
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: By the univalence principle for functions, the data above is contractible.

For any precategory C, the type \mathchoice {\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen D : \mathsf {Precategory}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen D : \mathsf {Precategory}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen D : \mathsf {Precategory}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen D : \mathsf {Precategory}\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{D\cong C} of isomorphisms of precategories onto C is contractible.

The type of isomorphisms onto C is inhabited, considering the identity isomorphism 1_{C}\colon C\cong C. Therefore, it suffices to show that the type of isomorphisms onto C depicted below is a proposition:
We unfold the definitions of a precategories and their isomorphisms, dropping propositional axioms:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {aligned} D_0&: \mathsf {Type}\\ D_1&:D_0\to D_0\to \mathsf {Set}\\ F_0&: D_0\to C_0\\ F_1&: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} D_1xy\to C \mathopen {}\left \lparen F_0x,F_0y\right \rparen \mathclose {} \\ h_1 &: \mathsf {isBiinvertible}\,F_0\\ h_2 &: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathsf {isPropMap} \mathopen {}\left \lparen F_1xy\right \rparen \mathclose {} \\ h_3 &: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} \mathsf {isSurjection} \mathopen {}\left \lparen F_1xy\right \rparen \mathclose {} \end {aligned}
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: A function that is both propositional and a surjection, i.e. a bijection, is an equivalence by . We can therefore repackage our telescope equivalently:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {aligned} D_0&: \mathsf {Type}\\ D_1&:D_0\to D_0\to \mathsf {Set}\\ F_0&: D_0\cong C_0\\ F_1&: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y: D_0\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} D_1xy\cong C \mathopen {}\left \lparen F_0x,F_0y\right \rparen \mathclose {} \end {aligned}

\begin {aligned} D_1&: \mathopen {}\left \lvert C\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathopen {}\left \lvert C\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathsf {Set}\\ F_1&: \mathchoice {\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in C\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in C\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in C\right \rparen \mathclose {} }}{\scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\in C\right \rparen \mathclose {} }} D_1xy\cong C \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\right \rparen \mathclose {} \end {aligned}
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Next we rewrite using the univalence principles for functions and families of types (, ):
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {aligned} D_1&: \mathopen {}\left \lvert C\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathopen {}\left \lvert C\right \rvert \mathclose {} \to \mathsf {Set}\\ F_1&: D_1 = \lambda {x,y}\mathpunct {.}{C \mathopen {}\left \lparen x,y\right \rparen \mathclose {} } \end {aligned}
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: By the univalence principle for functions, the data above is contractible.
